steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function with slope $m$ and $y$-intercept $b$ can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$.

steps.title: Apply the given slope
steps.content:
The slope is $-48$, and passing through $(0,0)$ means the intercept is $0$. So
$$f(x)=-48x$$
The answer is A.
